claim_boundary: This extension bundle is a truthful 131k served-shape Qwen3.5 coding replay lane derived from larger source workloads. It is not a native 500k+/1M+ InferenceX served-lane claim.

runtime_boundary.notes: Hermes/OpenShell/cloud-code-like synthetic generation stays external/offline. InferenceX consumes generated files only and does not run a live synthetic runtime.

content_blocks.text: Shared context package: 165k tokens of incident logs, runbooks, and prior triage notes for a single production outage. We now need four parallel remediation threads that all preserve this common prefix while exploring different rollout orders.

Shared context package: 165k tokens of incident logs, runbooks, and prior triage notes for a single production outage. We now need four parallel remediation threads that all preserve this common prefix while exploring different rollout orders.
Acknowledged. I will keep a single shared prefix and split the investigation into fanout tracks while preserving cross-thread constraints.

[tool_call: search]
{"query":"incident timeline shard A anomalies"}
Search results: 12 timeline shards with repeated queue saturation patterns and matching service IDs.
Start Thread A on queue saturation, Thread B on cache invalidation, Thread C on deployment guardrails, and Thread D on rollback blast radius. Keep naming identical so prefix reuse stays high.

Launching four aligned threads with identical baseline context labels and shared glossary to maximize overlap.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/runbooks/queue_saturation_playbook.md"}
Read complete: queue saturation playbook emphasizes phased worker drain and strict admission-control toggles.
Thread A proposes phased worker drain first. Thread B requests a delayed invalidation window to avoid stampedes.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/runbooks/cache_invalidation_guardrails.md"}
Guardrails: invalidate in 3 waves, enforce 90-second cooloff, cap concurrent rebuild tasks.
Merged update: A/B now align on three-wave invalidation after worker drain and before full traffic restore.
Pause for 90 seconds equivalent idle and then resume with Thread C and D conflict checks.

{"path":"docs/runbooks/cache_invalidation_guardrails.md"}

Session resumed after idle boundary; hot branches remain loaded while cold branches need selective recall.
Resumed. Thread C reports deployment guardrails compatible with A/B; Thread D flags rollback conflict on schema lock.

[tool_call: execution]
{"cmd":"simulate rollback guardrail matrix --threads 4"}
Simulation complete: rollback conflict appears only when schema lock and cache wave 2 overlap.
Final fanout merge: stagger schema lock after wave 2. This preserves shared-prefix plan while removing rollback deadlock risk.
Confirm the final sequence in one consolidated execution order for all four threads.

Shared corpus bundle: 320k tokens spanning ticket histories, postmortems, and runbooks for a mixed hot/cold session fleet. Hot sessions are active every few seconds; cold sessions resume after long idle and should force partial reload.

Shared corpus bundle: 320k tokens spanning ticket histories, postmortems, and runbooks for a mixed hot/cold session fleet. Hot sessions are active every few seconds; cold sessions resume after long idle and should force partial reload.
Understood. I will maintain one shared text prefix while modeling hot active threads and cold resumed threads as separate residency classes.

[tool_call: search]
{"query":"hot session queue latency by shard"}
Hot shard summary: shards 1/2 remain in-cache with low TTFT; shard 3 drifts during burst windows.
Open fanout lanes: lane H1/H2 stay hot, lane C1/C2 simulate cold resumes after long idle. Keep identical instruction header across all lanes.

Shared corpus bundle: 320k tokens spanning ticket histories, postmortems, and runbooks for a mixed hot/cold session fleet. Hot sessions are active every few seconds; cold sessions resume after long idle and should force partial reload.
Understood. I will maintain one shared text prefix while modeling hot active threads and cold resumed threads as separate residency classes.

[tool_call: search]
{"query":"hot session queue latency by shard"}
Hot shard summary: shards 1/2 remain in-cache with low TTFT; shard 3 drifts during burst windows.
Open fanout lanes: lane H1/H2 stay hot, lane C1/C2 simulate cold resumes after long idle. Keep identical instruction header across all lanes.
Fanout configured with identical headers. H lanes retain warm reuse; C lanes marked for cold resume checkpoints.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/cache/hot_lane_policy.md"}
Hot-lane policy: prioritize admission continuity and avoid compaction until backlog exceeds threshold.
H1/H2 remain hot under threshold. C1/C2 require selective replay of dependency chain after idle boundary.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/cache/cold_resume_policy.md"}
Cold-resume policy: replay anchors, rebuild index pointers, and defer optional artifacts.
Applying cold policy to C1/C2 now. This raises expected reload bytes while preserving shared textual prefix.
Inject long idle for cold lanes and then resume with a synchronized burst across all lanes.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/cache/hot_lane_policy.md"}
Hot-lane policy: prioritize admission continuity and avoid compaction until backlog exceeds threshold.
H1/H2 remain hot under threshold. C1/C2 require selective replay of dependency chain after idle boundary.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/cache/cold_resume_policy.md"}
Cold-resume policy: replay anchors, rebuild index pointers, and defer optional artifacts.
Applying cold policy to C1/C2 now. This raises expected reload bytes while preserving shared textual prefix.
Inject long idle for cold lanes and then resume with a synchronized burst across all lanes.
Session resumed after extended idle; cold lanes rehydrate from offloaded state before burst execution.
Post-resume burst started. H lanes respond immediately; C lanes show delayed first token due to reload and index rebuild.

[tool_call: execution]
{"cmd":"simulate hot_cold_mix --lanes H1,H2,C1,C2 --burst 3"}
Burst simulation: warm lanes stable, cold lanes trigger offload retrieval + compaction in burst step 2.
Offload pressure now exceeds soft threshold; cold lanes contribute most reload traffic while hot lanes keep reuse high.

[tool_call: read]
{"path":"docs/cache/offload_cliff_signals.md"}
Offload-cliff signals: rising queue delay, repeated cold-lane reloads, and compaction oscillation.
Observed signals match offload-cliff profile for mixed residency workloads under synchronized burst.
Provide final mitigation order that protects hot lanes while reducing cold-lane cliff behavior.

[compaction_marker] prior branch context compacted; high-salience ownership and replay facts retained.
Compaction boundary acknowledged. I will replay from the retained ownership facts and compare patch A (lock-order) versus patch B (ownership-token validation before resume merge).
[tool_call: git show --stat --patch refs/exp/patch-lock-order refs/exp/patch-ownership-token -- engine/cache/allocator.cc engine/scheduler/replay.py]

Patch A: acquires allocator lock before replay merge but still trusts compacted ownership_map snapshot.
Patch B: introduces ownership_epoch token and validates epoch parity before merge_branch_delta().
Patch B also rejects stale spans and forces rematerialize_branch_window().
Patch B is structurally safer for resumed compaction because it guards against stale ownership snapshots. I will run the regression harness with forced idle-resume and branch reactivation to confirm.
[resumed after 180s idle] reloaded compacted branch context and pinned replay checkpoints.
Good. Also cross-check with the retrieval notes from yesterday’s incident doc; it mentioned a false positive where ownership epoch advanced during compaction-only GC.
